title: Memorial to Star Wars creators
content:
{{Top|real|sprot|title=Memorial to ''Star Wars'' creators}}
{{Citation}}<!-- Missing citation for their works -->
[[File:Memorymoths.jpg|thumb|right|300px]]
This page is a '''memorial to ''Star Wars'' creators''', and serves as a tribute to the people who took part in the elaboration of the ''Star Wars'' universe.

==Years==
===Deaths in [[1978]]===
*[[Leigh Brackett]], who wrote the first draft of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], passed away from cancer aged 62 on [[March 18]], 1978.<ref name="Leigh Brackett">{{WebCite|url=http://womensf.loa.org/leigh-brackett|text=Leigh Brackett|work=[http://womensf.loa.org The Future is Female!]|archivedate=20220928222058}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1979]]===
*[[John Barry]], the production designer for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] and second unit director for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], passed away from meningitis aged 43 on [[June 1]], 1979, two weeks into production of the latter.<ref name="John Barry, Designer, Won Academy Award For 'star Wars' Film">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/1979/06/02/archives/john-barry-designer-won-academy-award-for-star-wars-film-made.html|text=John Barry, Designer, Won Academy Award For 'star Wars' Film|date=1979-06-02|archivedate=20230410015400}}</ref>
*[[John Wayne]], who voiced [[Garindan ezz Zavor]] in {{Film|IV}}, passed away from stomach cancer aged 72 on [[June 11]], 1979.<ref name="John Wayne Dead of Cancer on Coast at 72">{{WebCite|url=https://archive.nytimes.com/www.nytimes.com/books/97/03/23/reviews/wayne-obit.html|text=John Wayne Dead of Cancer on Coast at 72|date=1979-06-12|archivedate=20230727064559}}</ref>
*[[Jeff Moon]], who portrayed [[Damono Deomaley]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away aged 25 on [[October 30]], 1979.<ref name="Moon, Geoffrey">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nerf-herders-anonymous.com/p/moon-geoffrey.html|text=Moon, Geoffrey|archivedate=20230130053041}}</ref>
*[[Graham Ashley]], who portrayed [[Davish Krail|Gold Five]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away aged 52 on [[October 15]], 1979.<ref name="Graham Ashley">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=623|text=Graham Ashley|archivedate=20220528013703}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1981]]===
*[[Eddie Byrne]], who portrayed General [[Vanden Huyck Willard|Vanden Willard]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away from a stroke aged 70 on [[August 21]], 1981.<ref name="Eddie Byrne">{{WebCite|url=http://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=21369|text=Eddie Byrne|archivedate=20220420202054}}</ref>
*[[Russ Manning]], who wrote and penciled the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' newspaper comic strip]] in 1979 and 1980, passed away from colon cancer aged 52 on [[December 1]], 1981.<ref name="Russ Manning: Life and Several Works">{{Blogspot|subdomain=old-fashionedcomics|url=2019/04/russ-manning-biography-and-various-works.html|name=Old-fashioned Comics|text=Russ Manning: Life and Several Works|date=2019-04-27}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1982]]===
*[[Alan Webb]], who was originally slated to portray [[Darth Sidious|Emperor Palpatine]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] before bowing out and being replaced by [[Ian McDiarmid]], passed away aged 75 on [[June 22]], 1982.<ref name="Alan Webb">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=18081|text=Alan Webb|archivedate=20220613151135}}</ref>
*[[Gene Day]], a comic book inker and penciller, who worked on several of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] comic books, passed away from heart disease aged 31 on [[September 23]], [[1982]].<ref name="Gene Day -- 40 Years Gone">{{WebCite|url=https://dmrbooks.com/test-blog/2022/9/24/gene-day-40-years-gone|text=Gene Day -- 40 Years Gone|date=2022-09-24|archivedate=20220928164623}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1986]]===
*[[Marjorie Eaton]], who portrayed Emperor Palpatine in the pre-DVD version of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], passed away from a stroke aged 85 on [[April 21]], 1986.<ref name="Marjorie Eaton">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=34592|text=Marjorie Eaton|archivedate=20220628062108}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1987]]===
*[[Richard Marquand]], who directed [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], passed away from a heart attack aged 49 on [[September 4]], 1987.<ref name="RICHARD MARQUAND,MOVIEMAKER, DIES">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/1987/09/06/obituaries/richard-marquandmoviemaker-dies.html|text=RICHARD MARQUAND,MOVIEMAKER, DIES|date=1987-09-06|archivedate=20220503163354}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1988]]===
*[[Barry Gnome]], who portrayed [[Kabe]] in ''A New Hope'', passed away aged 74 on [[March 26]], 1988.<ref name="Barry Gnome">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=25318|text=Barry Gnome|archivedate=20181105110236}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1989]]===
*[[Claude Woolman]], an actor who played an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (wallscreen)|Imperial officer]] on a [[wallscreen]] in [[1978]]'s ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'', passed away on [[July 5]], 1989.<ref name="Claude Woolman IMDB">{{IMDb|name/nm0941263|text=Claude Woolman}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1990]]===
*[[Alex McCrindle]], who portrayed [[Jan Dodonna]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away after a short illness aged 78 on [[April 20]], 1990.<ref name="McCrindle Alex">{{WebCite|url=https://grahamstevenson.me.uk/2008/09/19/alex-mccrindle|text=McCrindle Alex|date=2008-09-19|archivedate=20221210091359}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1991]]===
*[[Vince Colletta]], who inked [[Star Wars (1977) 64|''Star Wars'' (1977) 64]], passed away from cancer aged 67 on [[June 3]], 1991.<ref name="The Deaths of the 20th Century Comic Book Greats by Alex Grand">{{WebCite|url=https://comicbookhistorians.com/the-deaths-of-the-20th-century-comic-book-greats-by-alex-grand|text=The Deaths of the 20th Century Comic Book Greats by Alex Grand|date=2018-04-18|archivedate=20190908162841}}</ref>
*[[Rick Cimino]], who voiced [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] in the second season of [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], passed away aged 61 on [[November 10]], 1991.<ref name="Richard Angelo  Cimino">{{WebCite|url=https://prabook.com/web/richard_angelo.cimino/612506|text=Richard Angelo  Cimino|archivedate=20211022140904}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1992]]===
*[[Graeme Campbell]], who provided voices for the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']] animated series, passed away from cancer aged 51 on [[January 18]], 1992.<ref name="Graeme Campbell">{{WebCite|url=https://digiguide.tv/celebrity/Graeme+Campbell/5331|text=Graeme Campbell|archivedate=20200920143447}}</ref>
*[[Anthony Lang]], who portrayed [[Sim Aloo]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], passed away on [[March 2]], 1992.<ref name="Anthony Lang">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=25327|text=Anthony Lang|archivedate=20221203004845}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1994]]===
*[[William Conrad]], who narrated the original 1977 television broadcast of ''[[The Making of Star Wars]]'', passed away from a heart attack aged 73 on [[February 11]], 1994.<ref name="Obituary: William Conrad">{{WebCite|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/obituary-william-conrad-1394098.html|text=Obituary: William Conrad|date=1994-02-14|archivedate=20221210104749}}</ref>
*[[Peter Cushing]], who portrayed [[Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away from prostate cancer aged 81 on [[August 11]], 1994.<ref name="Obituary: Peter Cushing">{{WebCite|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/obituary-peter-cushing-1375859.html|text=Obituary: Peter Cushing|date=1994-08-11|archivedate=20221019214653}}</ref>
*[[Sydney Walker]], who voiced [[Deej Warrick]] in ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'' and ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]'', passed away from cancer aged 73 on [[September 30]], 1994.<ref name="Sydney Walker, 73, A Character Actor Of Stage and Film">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/1994/10/29/obituaries/sydney-walker-73-a-character-actor-of-stage-and-film.html|text=Sydney Walker, 73, A Character Actor Of Stage and Film|date=1994-10-29|archivedate=20220113084159}}</ref>
*[[Tarik]], a [[Wikipedia:Black bear|black bear]] who was the primary source for the voice of [[Chewbacca]], passed away from congestive heart failure aged 16 during [[October]] 1994.<ref name="SWI">{{InsiderCite|24|StarNews}}</ref>
*[[Sebastian Shaw]], who portrayed [[Anakin Skywalker]] in the original version of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], passed away from natural causes aged 89 on [[December 23]], 1994.<ref name="Sebastian Shaw">{{WebCite|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/sebastian-shaw-1572845.html|text=Sebastian Shaw|date=1995-02-13|archivedate=20221225220814}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1995]]===
*[[Pat Welsh]], who voiced [[Boushh]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], passed away from pneumonia aged on [[January 26]], 1995.<ref name="Pat Welsh">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=74328|text=Pat Welsh|archivedate=20210803111343}}</ref>
*[[Burl Ives]], who narrated ''[[Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure]]'', passed away from oral cancer aged 85 on [[April 14]], 1995.<ref name="Obituary: Burl Ives">{{WebCite|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/obituary-burl-ives-1616104.html|text=Obituary: Burl Ives|date=1995-04-17|archivedate=20220706174236}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1996]]===
*[[Brian Daley]], who authored ''[[The Han Solo Adventures]]'' and the [[Star Wars (radio)|''Star Wars'' radio dramas]], passed away from pancreatic cancer aged 48 on [[February 11]], 1996.<ref name="Information on Brian Daley">{{WebCite|url=https://brian-daley.com/BrianDaley-bio.htm|text=Information on Brian Daley|archivedate=20230413043421}}</ref>
*[[Jeremy Sinden]], who portrayed [[Dex Tiree]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away from lung cancer aged 45 on [[May 29]], 1996.<ref name="Obituary: Jeremy Sinden">{{WebCite|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/people/obituary-jeremy-sinden-1349970.html|text=Obituary: Jeremy Sinden|date=1996-05-31|archivedate=20230603135414}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1997]]===
*[[Don Henderson]], who portrayed General [[Cassio Tagge/Legends|Cassio Tagge]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away from throat cancer aged 65 on [[June 22]], 1997.<ref name="Don Henderson">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=7810|text=Don Henderson|archivedate=20221209034547}}</ref>
*[[Jack Purvis]], who portrayed [[Nebit|the]] Chief [[Jawa]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], an [[Ugnaught]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], passed away aged 60 on [[November 21]], 1997.<ref name="Jack Purvis">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=14156|text=Jack Purvis|archivedate=20220613150912}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1998]]===
*[[Archie Goodwin]], the writer of numerous stories for the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' newspaper comic strip]] and the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series, passed away from cancer aged 60 on [[March 1]], 1998.<ref name="The Best Loved Man In Comics: A Birthday Tribute To Archie Goodwin">{{WebCite|url=https://comicsalliance.com/tribute-archie-goodwin|text=The Best Loved Man In Comics: A Birthday Tribute To Archie Goodwin|date=2015-09-08|archivedate=20230324194406}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[1999]]===
*[[Billy J. Mitchell]], who portrayed [[Keir Santage]] in {{Film|VI}}, passed away from cancer aged 58 circa [[March]] 1999.<ref name="Billy J. Mitchell">{{BookCite|container=In-demand actor dies|title=[[Wikipedia:Reading Post|''Reading Reading Post'']]|date=1999-03-10}} via {{WebCite|url=https://www.newspapers.com/article/reading-evening-post-billy-j-mitchell-ob/109973530|text=Billy J Mitchell Obituary|work=[[Wikipedia:Ancestry.com|Newspapers.com]]|archivedate=20230816165833}}</ref>
*[[Declan Mulholland]], the stand-in for [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure|Jabba the Hutt]] in ''[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope]]'', passed away from a heart attack aged 66 on [[June 29]], 1999.<ref name="Declan Mulholland">{{WebCite|url=https://www.theguardian.com/news/1999/jul/27/guardianobituaries1|text=Declan Mulholland|date=1999-07-27|archivedate=20211008215948}}</ref>
*[[Morris Bush]], who portrayed [[Dengar]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], passed away in [[August]] 1999.<ref name="Maurice Bush">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=2428|text=Maurice Bush|archivedate=20220613151053}}</ref>
*[[Mary Kay Bergman]], who voiced 5 characters in the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' video game]], passed away at age 38 on [[November 11]], 1999.<ref name="Mary Kay Bergman">{{WebCite|url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-1999-nov-21-me-36014-story.html|text=Mary Kay Bergman; Voice Actress in 'South Park'|date=1999-11-21|archivedate=20230422214812}}</ref>
*[[Edvin Biuković]], the penciller for ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: The Phantom Affair]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: The Last Command]]'', passed away from a brain tumor aged 30 on [[December 5]], 1999.<ref name="Edvin Biukovic">{{DarkHorse|url=Press-Releases/323/Edvin-Biukovic-1969-1999|text=Edvin Biukovic 1969-1999}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[2000]]===
*[[Gil Kane]], an illustrator for [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']], passed away from lymphoma aged 73 on [[January 31]], 2000.<ref name="Gil Kane">{{WebCite|url=https://www.theguardian.com/news/2000/feb/04/guardianobituaries|text=Gil Kane|date=2000-02-04|archivedate=20230405143303}}</ref>
*[[George Roussos]], an illustrator for [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']], passed away from a heart attack aged 84 on [[February 19]], 2000.<ref name="The Art and Life of George Roussos">{{WebCite|url=http://www.meskin.net/roussos.html|text=The Art and Life of George Roussos|archivedate=20170928175523}}</ref>
*[[Alfredo Alcala]], an illustrator of many Classic Star Wars comics, passed away from cancer aged 74 on [[April 8]], 2000.<ref name="Obituary: Alfredo Alcala, 1925-2000">{{WebCite|url=https://www.comicsreporter.com/index.php/resources/longbox/2073|text=Obituary: Alfredo Alcala, 1925-2000|date=2000-05-30|archivedate=20221210081246}}</ref>
*[[Chic Stone]], who inked [[Star Wars (1977) 45|''Star Wars'' (1977) 45]], passed away aged 77 on [[July 28]], 2000.<ref name="Chic Stone (b. 1923)">{{WebCite|url=https://www.comics.org/creator/2188|text=Chic Stone (b. 1923)|archivedate=20230218101838}}</ref>
*Sir [[Alec Guinness]], who portrayed [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars:'' Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], as well as posthumously appearing through archive audio in [[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|''Star Wars:'' Episode VII ''The Force Awakens'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars:'' Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']], passed away from liver cancer aged 86 on [[August 5]], 2000.<ref name="Sir Alec Guinness">{{WebCite|url=https://www.theguardian.com/news/2000/aug/08/guardianobituaries.tomsutcliffe|text=Sir Alec Guinness|date=2000-08-08|archivedate=20230321161047}}</ref>
*[[Shelagh Fraser]], who portrayed [[Beru Whitesun Lars]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away aged 79 on [[August 29]], 2000.<ref name="Shelagh Fraser">{{WebCite|url=https://www.theguardian.com/news/2000/sep/20/guardianobituaries2|text=Shelagh Fraser|date=2000-09-20|archivedate=20221129065039}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[2001]]===
*[[Tom Chantrell]], who designed the ''Style C'' posters for ''Star Wars'' in 1977, passed away from a heart attack aged 84 on [[July 15]], 2001.<ref name="Tom Chantrell">{{WebCite|url=https://www.theguardian.com/news/2001/jul/28/guardianobituaries.filmnews|text=Tom Chantrell|date=2001-07-28|archivedate=20230604155502}}</ref>
*[[Ted Burnett]], who portrayed [[Wuher/Legends|Wuher]] the bartender in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away from a heart attack aged 74 on [[October 1]], 2001.<ref name="Ted Burnett">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=25310|text=Ted Burnett|archivedate=20210227014613}}</ref>
*[[Anne Polland]], a costume-maker for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars:'' Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], passed away aged 54 on [[November 1]], 2001.<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.sfgate.com/news/article/Anne-Marie-Polland-2858546.php|text=Anne Marie Polland|date=2001-10-11|archiveurl=https://archive.is/wip/JT6og}}</ref>
*[[Francis Batsoni]], who portrayed a [[Unidentified moisture farmer (Mos Eisley)|moisture farmer]] in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] as well as an [[Unidentified Imperial Advisor (tall hat)|Imperial Advisor]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], passed away aged 89 on [[December 12]], 2001.<ref name="Francis Batsoni">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=171247|text=Francis Batsoni|archivedate=20181105081400}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[2002]]===
*[[Roy Conrad]], who portrayed [[Ace Merrick]] in ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'', passed away from lung cancer aged 61 on [[January 19]], 2002.<ref name="Roy Conrad">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=3509|text=Roy Conrad|archivedate=20201127084934}}</ref>
*[[Claire Davenport]], who portrayed [[Yarna d'al' Gargan]] in Episode VI, passed away from kidney failure aged 68 on [[February 25]], 2002.<ref name="Claire Davenport">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=4219|text=Claire Davenport|archivedate=20221113012432}}</ref>
*[[George Alec Effinger]], who wrote ''[[The Great God Quay: The Tale of Barada and the Weequays]]'', passed away from an internal haemorrhage aged 55 on [[April 27]], 2002.<ref name="George Effinger, 55, Who Laced Science Fiction With Dark Humor">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2002/05/02/arts/george-effinger-55-who-laced-science-fiction-with-dark-humor.html|text=George Effinger, 55, Who Laced Science Fiction With Dark Humor|date=2002-05-02|archivedate=20230516155755}}</ref>
*[[Des Webb]], who portrayed [[One-Arm|the]] [[wampa]] in Episode V, passed away from a stroke aged 67 on [[May 21]], 2002.<ref name="Des Webb">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=25548|text=Des Webb|archivedate=20220613150721}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[2003]]===
*[[Reginald Thomason]], who portrayed a cantina patron and a rebel technician in {{Film|IV}}, passed away aged 84 on [[August 30]], 2003.<ref name="Reg Thomason">{{WebCite|url=https://aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=40004|text=Reg Thomason|archivedate=20230528035905}}</ref>
*[[Anne Gerety]], who portrayed [[Beru Whitesun Lars]] in the [[Star Wars (radio)|''Star Wars'' radio drama]], passed away from a heart attack aged 77 on [[October 25]], 2003.<ref name="Anne  Gerety">{{WebCite|url=https://prabook.com/web/anne.gerety/2588505|text=Anne  Gerety|archivedate=20230831134955}}</ref>
*[[Gilda Cohen]], who portrayed a cantina patron in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], passed away aged 83 on [[November 5]], 2003.<ref name="Gilda Cohen">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=80666|text=Gilda Cohen|archivedate=20181105110246}}</ref>
*[[Art Carney]], who portrayed [[Saun Dann]] in ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'', passed away from natural causes aged 85 on [[November 9]], 2003.<ref name="Art Carney, 85, Lauded 'Honeymooners' Actor, Dies">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2003/11/12/business/art-carney-85-lauded-honeymooners-actor-dies.html|text=Art Carney, 85, Lauded 'Honeymooners' Actor, Dies|date=2003-11-12|archivedate=20221004144640}}</ref>
*[[John Tartaglione]], who inked [[Star Wars Weekly 58|''Star Wars Weekly'' 58]] and [[Star Wars Weekly 59|''Star Wars Weekly'' 59]], passed away from throat cancer aged 82 on [[November 12]], 2003.<ref name="John Tartaglione">{{WebCite|url=https://www.lambiek.net/artists/t/tartaglione_john.htm|text=John Tartaglione|date=2018-07-05|archivedate=20230213205422}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[2004]]===
*[[Peter Diamond]], who was the stunt coordinator on [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], assisted with stunts in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], and also played stormtroopers, Tusken Raiders and a snowtrooper throughout the trilogy, passed away from a stroke aged 74 on [[March 27]], 2004.<ref name="Peter Diamond">{{WebCite|url=https://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=4626|text=Peter Diamond|archivedate=20221002042106}}</ref>
*[[Bruce Boa]], who portrayed [[General]] [[Carlist Rieekan|Rieekan]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], passed away from cancer 73 on [[April 17]], 2004.<ref name="From Terry the chef to Miss Tibbs: As we bid farewell to our beloved Manuel, what became of the rest of the Fawlty Towers cast?">{{WebCite|url=https://www.dailymail.co.uk/femail/article-3993528/From-Terry-chef-Miss-Tibbs-bid-farewell-beloved-Manuel-rest-Fawlty-Towers-cast-now.html|text=From Terry the chef to Miss Tibbs: As we bid farewell to our beloved Manuel, what became of the rest of the Fawlty Towers cast?|date=2016-12-02|archivedate=20210506171602}}</ref>

===Deaths in [[2005]]===
*[[Frank Kelly Freas]], who worked as an illustrator for some ''Star Wars'' projects, passed away from natural causes aged 82 on [[January 2]], 2005.<ref name="Frank Kelly Freas">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2005/01/05/arts/design/f-k-freas-who-drew-the-devilish-face-of-mad-magazine-dies-at-82.html|text=F. K. Freas, Who Drew the Devilish Face of Mad Magazine, Dies at 82|author=Douglas Martin|date=2005-01-05|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|''The New York Times'']]|archivedate=20230705172127}}</ref>
*[[Long John Baldry]], the voice of the [[Great Heep]] in the [[1986]] television film ''[[The Great Heep]]'', passed away in [[Wikipedia:Vancouver|Vancouver]] on [[July 21]], 2005, following a four-month battle with a severe chest infection.<ref name="CBC">{{WebCite|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/british-columbia/long-john-baldry-dies-in-vancouver-1.544973|text=Long John Baldry dies in Vancouver|work=[[Wikipedia:CBC News|CBC News]]|date=2005-07-22|archivedate=20251031171609}}</ref>
*[[Alf Joint]], a stunt performer who worked on [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], passed away from cancer aged 77 on [[July 25]], 2005.<ref name="Alf Joint (1927-2005)">{{WebCite|url=https://fanderson.org.uk/alf-joint-1927-2005|text=Alf Joint (1927-2005)|archivedate=20220529030311}}</ref>
